executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture ownership and verifier freshness remain repaired, but full signoff stays fail-closed on the marketing primary-repo adoption watchpoint.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Keep architecture locally green only as a qualified pass until marketing clears the primary-repo adoption measurement window or replaces the tactic.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and verifier refreshes
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing adoption watchpoint remains the only real live blocker
findings.mechanism: The architecture path is locally healthy, while the marketing loop is still intentionally fail-closed on flat primary-repo adoption inside its active measurement window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for the marketing owner loop is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Do not certify around the watchpoint; wait for measurable Codeberg movement or a marketing-owned tactic replacement at the review window.

findings.title: Architecture verifier now classifies marketing review-followups as external watchpoints instead of local blockers
findings.mechanism: Fresh health-monitor follow-up items from the marketing loop no longer poison architecture signoff during the same refresh cycle.
findings.root_cause: Marketing review-followup issues were previously treated as architecture-owned blockers even when they were just downstream fail-closed evidence.
findings.recommended_fix: Preserve the external-watchpoint classification for marketing follow-up artifacts so architecture signoff tracks the real owner boundary.

findings.title: Persisted disabled cron history still exists but is not a live-topology problem
findings.mechanism: jobs.json contains disabled historical entries while the live Gateway topology has zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Persisted history and live scheduler state are distinct surfaces and must stay reported separately.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live enabled runtime jobs in all architecture summaries.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture signoff tied to the external marketing blocker and not to derivative runner/verifier redness inside that owner loop
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The architecture path is repaired again and should stay localized to the real blocker.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: No bogus architecture incident while the real blocker remains marketing-owned.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Resolve the marketing measurement window with measurable primary-repo movement or tactic replacement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Codeberg adoption is still flat across the active window.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either a marketing pass artifact or a concrete replacement path owned by marketing.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Reran loop_integrity_audit.py, refreshed the architecture latest report, then reran agent_architecture_independent_verify.py, agent_architecture_verifier.py, and agent_architecture_checker.py against the live Gateway topology.
Updated the latest architecture report to reflect that the only live blocker is the marketing primary-repo adoption measurement window, not an architecture-owned verifier/runtime fault.
Resynced live cron counts, persisted disabled-history separation, current health-monitor issue set, and verifier timestamps before signoff.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass after primary-repo adoption moves or the current tactic is replaced at the end of the measurement window.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Primary Codeberg adoption is still flat under a live marketing measurement window
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: That is still the only blocker preventing a fully green end-to-end certification.
